_meta.methodology: Labour Force Survey (LFS). ILO unemployment definition: people without a job who have been actively seeking work in the past four weeks and are available to start in the next two weeks. Economic inactivity: people not in employment and not seeking or available for work.

unemployment_rate.label: Unemployment rate
unemployment_rate.note: ILO definition. Applies to people aged 16-64.

economic_inactivity.label: Economic inactivity rate
economic_inactivity.note: People not working and not looking for work. Includes students, carers, long-term sick. Rarely discussed but has risen significantly since pandemic — 9 million people.

real_wages_growth.label: Real wages growth (year on year)
real_wages_growth.note: Wages adjusted for inflation. Positive for first time in two years, but real wages remain below 2008 peak.

child_poverty.label: Children in relative poverty
child_poverty.note: Relative poverty = household income below 60% of median. After housing costs. Around 4.3 million children.
